mnemonics: ANOVA: Analysis Of Variance Assesses - Compares means between groups by analyzing variance within and between them.
Remember the F-Ratio: Between Group Variance / Within Group Variance. Think 'F for Fraction' of between over within.
SS Stands for Sum of Squares - SST (Total) = SSB (Between) + SSW (Within). Like a pie (SST) split into group (SSB) and error (SSW) pieces.
keyPoints: ANOVA tests the hypothesis that the means of three or more groups are equal.
The F-statistic is the key test statistic, calculated as (Variance Between Groups) / (Variance Within Groups).
Rejecting the null hypothesis only tells you that not all group means are equal; post-hoc tests are needed to find which specific pairs differ.
commonMistakes: Assuming a significant ANOVA result means all group means are different from each other, when it only indicates that at least one is different.
Failing to check the assumptions of ANOVA: normality of data, homogeneity of variances, and independence of observations.
Confusing the Sum of Squares Between (SSB) with the Sum of Squares Within (SSW) when setting up the ANOVA table calculations.
